title: colocasia antiquorum var. nymphaeifolia
intro: Colocasia antiquorum var. nymphaeifolia, also known as the Nymphaea-leafed elephant ear or simply Nymphaea-leafed colocasia, is a variety of the Colocasia antiquorum plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Arum. This plant is known for its large, flat leaves that resemble those of the water lily, hence its name, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves of Colocasia antiquorum var. nymphaeifolia have been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Colocasia antiquorum var. nymphaeifolia include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, burns, and skin irritations.
